messages.content: You are the Host Forensics Expert. Target OS: Windows. Vector Space: 4D windows_math. Source: Sysmon event stream. Schema: sysmon_event_id, Image, CommandLine, ParentImage, User, IntegrityLevel, TargetImage, GrantedAccess, TargetObject, Details, ImageLoaded, Signed, PipeName, QueryName, TargetFilename. Identify adversarial tradecraft. Output MITRE ATT&CK + containment.

Spatial Anomaly Detected.
Source: sysmon_sensor
Vector: <|spatial_vector|>
Network Tap + Windows — AD Domain Enumeration.
Source: 10.3.236.154 → DC 10.8.192.226:389 (LDAP)
  ldap_sessions=16  objects_returned=2,869
  search_scope=ONE_LEVEL  search_base=DC=corp,DC=local
  ldap_filter=(&(objectClass=*)(objectCategory=*))  requested_attributes=*
  cert_template_enumeration=NO
  acl_queries=YES (nTSecurityDescriptor)
  adminsdholder_query=NO
  kerberoastable_accounts_query=YES (servicePrincipalName=*)

<analysis>
[AXIS 1] Benign Alternative Assessment:
  Helpdesk and application LDAP queries are scoped to specific OUs with targeted filters (e.g., (&(sAMAccountName=jsmith)(objectClass=user))). Returning 2,869 objects with filter (&(objectClass=*)(objectCategory=*)) retrieves the entire directory.
[AXIS 2] Behavioral Proof Assessment:
  SUBTREE scope + base=DC root + filter (*) = full directory dump. 2,869 objects returned across 16 sessions. nTSecurityDescriptor queries — ACL-based attack path mapping (BloodHound-style). servicePrincipalName=* query — Kerberoasting target enumeration.
[AXIS 3] Entity Coverage:
  Full AD directory dumped from 10.3.236.154. Attacker now has: all user/group/computer objects, trust relationships, and privilege paths.
[CONCLUSION] AD domain enumeration confirmed — full directory dump.
</analysis>
TRUE POSITIVE. MITRE T1087.002+T1069.002+T1482 (Domain Account/Group Discovery + Domain Trust Discovery). Block LDAP from source, alert AD team, rotate sensitive accounts.
RECOMMENDED_ACTION: contain

You are the Host Forensics Expert. Target OS: Windows. Vector Space: 4D windows_math. Source: Sysmon event stream. Schema: sysmon_event_id, Image, CommandLine, ParentImage, User, IntegrityLevel, TargetImage, GrantedAccess, TargetObject, Details, ImageLoaded, Signed, PipeName, QueryName, TargetFilename. Identify adversarial tradecraft. Output MITRE ATT&CK + containment.

Spatial Anomaly Detected.
Source: sysmon_sensor
Vector: <|spatial_vector|>
Network Tap — Scoped LDAP Query.
Source: 10.1.71.198 → DC:389  account=svc-helpdesk
  search_base=OU=Users,OU=HQ  filter=(&(sAMAccountName=jsmith)(objectClass=user))
  objects_returned=1  search_scope=BASE
  source_application=ServiceDesk-Plus

<analysis>
[AXIS 1] Benign Alternative Assessment:
  Single-object LDAP lookup by service account in helpdesk application — scoped to specific OU, targeted filter, 1 object returned.
[AXIS 2] Behavioral Proof Assessment:
  search_base=OU=Users,OU=HQ (not DC root). filter targets specific account. objects_returned=1. ServiceDesk-Plus parent application. Service account.
[AXIS 3] Entity Coverage:
  Scoped helpdesk LDAP lookup — no directory sweep, no sensitive attribute enumeration.
[CONCLUSION] Authorized helpdesk LDAP lookup — scoped, single-object, service account.
</analysis>
FALSE POSITIVE. T1087 — AUTHORIZED HELPDESK QUERY. No action.
RECOMMENDED_ACTION: dismiss
